member.name: Sarah Russell
member.synopsis: Sarah Russell is the Labour MP for Congleton , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Clean Energy: North West
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to help increase funding for clean energy in the North West.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is directly investing into the North-West, funding key initiatives such as the Hynet CCUS cluster, projects in our Hydrogen Allocation Rounds, the Green Heat Network Fund and Local Growth Fund. Great British Energy has funded solar panels a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Rape: Youth Courts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many rapes were tried in the youth court last year and in each of the preceding three years; and of those, for each year, (a) how many resulted in a guilty verdict, (b) how many were sentenced in the youth co
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice publishes magistrates’ court data on prosecutions and resulting outcomes for rape offences by age of the defendant. This can be downloaded from the Criminal Justice Statistics landing page here: Criminal Justice Statistics.Although...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Revenue and Customs: Forms
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether all HMRC forms are available in paper form on request.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMRC remains committed to its digital‑first strategy. As part of this, it is delivering a programme to move at least 75% of communications currently issued by paper to digital channels in the current spending review period. However, HMRC recognises that n...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Revenue and Customs: Forms
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of making all HMRC forms available (a) online and (b) in paper form.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMRC remains committed to its digital‑first strategy. As part of this, it is delivering a programme to move at least 75% of communications currently issued by paper to digital channels in the current spending review period. However, HMRC recognises that n...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what his planned timetable is for the publication of the 10-Year Workplace Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We will publish the 10 Year Workforce Plan imminently.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the impact of potholes on rural road safety.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 2024 (the latest year for which figures are available), an estimated 4% of reported personal injury road collisions on rural roads in Great Britain were assigned a contributory road safety factor of ‘poor or defective road surface or deposits on road’....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Medicine: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to increase the number of medical training places in Cheshire and Merseyside.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government committed in the 10-Year Health Plan to create 1,000 new specialty training posts over the next three years with a focus on specialties where there is the greatest need. Further information on which specialties and regions will receive thes...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to improve the availability of domestic abuse services in semi-rural areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Every victim of violence against women and girls (VAWG), whether in a city or a rural village, should be able to access the help they need. In the 'Freedom from Violence and Abuse’ Strategy, we committed to developing a new cross-government statement on t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to ensure the accessibility of new housing for people with disabilities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data relating to the number of wheelchair accessible homes is not collected centrally, although the English Housing Survey collects occasional series on accessibility and adaptations within the home. Between 16 December 2025 and 10 March 2026, the governm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he has made an assessment of the sufficiency of the level of accessible homes for people with disabilities in the North West.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data relating to the number of wheelchair accessible homes is not collected centrally, although the English Housing Survey collects occasional series on accessibility and adaptations within the home. Between 16 December 2025 and 10 March 2026, the governm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Migrant Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Home Office's White Paper entitled Restoring control over the immigration system, published on 12 May 2025, whether his Department has made an assessment of the potential impa
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has published impact assessments alongside the Spring 2025 Immigration Rules, which implement proposals set out in the White Paper, Restoring control over the immigration system. These impact assessments set out the expected effects of the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dentistry: Complaints
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what discussions he has had with the General Dental Council on reducing the waiting time for fitness to practice cases to be heard.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has regular discussions with the General Dental Council (GDC) on regulatory matters. While the GDC is an independent regulator responsible for managing its fitness to practise processes, the Government expects the GDC to take steps to impro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitals: Discharges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has made an assessment of the potential impact of reductions in the social care workforce on the level of delayed discharges from hospitals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made an assessment of the potential impact of reductions in the social care workforce on the level of delayed discharges from hospitals.Achieving timely and effective discharge for hospital patients relies on effective joint working...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Palliative Care: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has made an assessment of the long-term sustainability of the palliative care workforce.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Last year, we published our 10-year plan to deliver a National Health Service fit for the future and a central part of the plan is our workforce and how we ensure we train and provide the staff, technology, and infrastructure the NHS needs to care for pat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: STEM Subjects: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what measures her Department is taking to support women working in UK science technology, engineering and mathematic sectors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Science, Innovation and Technology recognises the importance of supporting women working in the UK science, technology, engineering and mathematic sectors.In December 2025, DSIT launched the Women in Tech Taskforce to address the system...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what his planned timetable is for publication of the final findings of the independent maternity services investigation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Baroness Amos has advised that the independent National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation will publish its final report and recommendations in June.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Paternity Leave
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent assessment his Department has made of the potential benefits of increasing the statutory entitlement to paternity leave.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the Employment Rights Act, we are making Paternity Leave a ‘day one’ right from 6 April this year. This will bring an extra 32,000 fathers and partners into scope of the entitlement. The Government also launched the Parental Leave and Pay Review o...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Conditions of Employment: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what progress his Department has made on improving parental rights for workers in (a) Congleton constituency and (b) Cheshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is dedicated to improving rights for working parents. In April, we are making Unpaid Parental Leave and Paternity Leave day-one rights, as well as introducing Bereaved Partners Paternity Leave. In 2027, we will further improve protections ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Students: Loans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate she has made on the number of people with a Plan 3 student loan in England and Wales; and what is the total value of those loans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The number of England‑domiciled borrowers with a Plan 3 student loan was 603,000, rounded to the nearest thousand, and the total value of those loans was £6.521 billion, rounded to the nearest million, as of 31 March 2025.Education is a devolved matter, a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthy Start Scheme: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will estimate how many four year-olds live in families that would be eligible for Healthy Start vouchers had their child not become ineligible on their fourth birthday.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Healthy Start scheme helps to encourage a healthy diet for pregnant women, babies, and young children under four years old from low-income households. The NHS Business Services Authority (NHSBSA) operates the Healthy Start scheme on behalf of the Depa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
